titleStmt.title: Translation of BL L.F.C. Ch. xxx.5

div.p: William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all the worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know me to have given and conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to John Waleram, in return for his homage and service, the whole land which William Carpenter held in Babbet, that is, every fifth rig of the whole half of Babbet, and in the fields of Drony, the land which Hugh Gifford, Alexander de St-Martin, Godfrey the marischal, Winemar, and other worthy men perambulated for Roger de Camera at the instruction of the Countess Ada, my mother; and in addition, the whole land in Airdrie [in Crail] that William de Beauver held, that is, the land which is on the east side of the burn that runs beside the land of Geoffrey the chaplain, as far as the well at Crail which in Gaelic is called 'Tolari' [Toldrie, in Crail]; and the whole land by the same boundaries which Geoffrey de Malville, Hugh Gifford, Godfre the marischal, Winemar and other worthy men perambulated for the aforesaid William on every side towards the moor and elsewhere; and common pasture at Crail; and one toft in Crail, that is, the one which the Countess Ada, my mother, gave to the aforesaid William. Wherefore I will and firmly enjoin that the aforesaid John and his heirs have and hold all the aforewritten holdings of me and my heirs in feu and heritage, in meadows and pastures, in moors and marshes, and in all other easements that belong by right to the aforesaid lands, freely and quitly, fully and honourably, by the service of one sergeand on a horse with a haubergel, and performing in aids and forinsec services in return for the whole land that he has in Airdrie as much service as pertains to half a Scottish ploughgate in Crailshire. I have also granted to the aforesaid John and his heirs that they be quit of multure for their own house at my mill, but that his men should give multure; and that the corn of John and his heirs be milled at my mill first after the corn that is in already in the hopper, unless my corn already be there or arrive in the meantime. As witnesses: Philip de Valognes, my chamberlain; William, my chaplain; William Comyn; Hugh and William and Adam, my clerks; Master Nicholas and Master Martin, my clerks; Alexander, sheriff of Stirling; William de Valognes; Alexander, son of Thor; John of Stirling; Robert Quarrantilly. At Clackmannan, on the 13th day of October.
